Narrative_Meaning: Kings dignify an older mature male, the patriarchy, business acumen, wisdom, knowledge and good advice.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Færy Noble card stands for the Lords of the Fae. It represents power, of an unearthly, paradoxical, fateful, magical, cruel or capricious nature. It can be a sign of erratic, irrational, or even insane seeming behaviour, but says there's a reason beneath. The Færy Noble can be involved in important historical events, but you are just as likely to find one engaged in something mundane (at least from their point of view) such as painting the dawn red to warn shepherds of rain. Then again sometimes the card represents a Leader or Mentor who is "(Fairy-)Touched(-in-the-head)". Here again, the irrationality may be signs of genius, prophecy, or insanity, or a mixture.

Significator.Plot: The Færy Noble Plot is a tale of imagination and fantasy. Any fantasy so fantastic that it may be a dream, is suitable for the Færy Noble. Madness and magic are common themes and you can expect reality warping Yarn-Telling as a common thread. Often these Fantasy events are tied to some idea of prophecy, karma or destiny.
Significator.Side: A side of a conflict marked by the Færy Noble are sometimes actually Fae, or time-travellers, trying to keep destiny or history on track.
Significator.Character:
The Færy Noble Significator indicates a powerful, capricious Character.

They Gain Yin by changing their mind.

They Gain Yang by using their Annexes.

They Gain Chi by utilizing deeper structures than logic, or linear time, which can appear as acting crazy to normal people.

They gain +1 Success Levels when Stakes are Paradoxical.
Significator.Scene: The Færy Noble Scene is one where the world is inverted, tipped upside down, or otherwise reordered. Trivial Events are magnified in importance and important events rendered pointless. Færy Nobles always seem to appear near great events (although are oddly focused on something else entirely). Yarn-Tellers may play the card to evoke magical, reality altering powers, travel to inner realms, dreams, or other worlds, or use it to summon time-travelling hyper-dimensional beings without human weaknesses like feelings or conscience (if you want).

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's I-Ching. For example: If a Character has the Cauldron (50) I-Ching, then the Hook needs to have someone hungry turn up asking for help.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Hook just consists of some enormous danger, it isn't looming, it isn't coming, it is right here, right now.
Yarn.Gain: Extremely Powerful Yarn-Teller Ally / Artefact Power Descendant
Yarn.Fray_Description: Maybe there's a Ward, a Runic barrier, an Alchemical Seal, or some dark and sinister Psibertek involved, but magic is in the Fray, and the PCs better have a mage on their side. Magic can come in a lot of different flavours, spells and cantrips, glamours and illusions, necromancy, scrying, summoning, invocations and evocations. This is usually a Low to Paradoxical Opposed, Co-operative or occasionally Solitary Ordeal.
Yarn.Snag_Description: The Fray is complicated by a changing or changeable situation. Perhaps a race is occurring in a tidal area, or perhaps the Villain is capricious and changes their plan during the middle of the Fray. Maybe the PCs goals should change mid-way through the Fray, as having promised to win, they are made to promise to lose or vice versa.

NPC.Description: Kings are Leaders who feel a Duty to protect their subjects not just lead them.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The PCs meet a Heirophant or Prophetess Mentor Character who can explain everything, and usually has some special ability the Character's will require. This is a combined Gain Ally/Training/Lore Descendant and Revelation. Think Obi-Wan or the Oracle.
Yarn.History_Description: The Færy Noble indicates that the history that leads to the current situation is somehow destined or pre-ordained somehow. It may simply be that the historical record includes portents, signs, omens or fore-shadowing of the current situation.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Færy Noble indicates that the current situation is one that would sound mad if you tried to explain it to someone not involved, this may be due to magic, temporal paradoxes, weird alternate dimensions, supernatural happenings or actual mental illness as required.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Færy Noble indicates that the solution to the current problem is to destabilise it somehow, this may be best achieved through illogical, erratic, capriciousness, impulsive, whimsical, or quirky behaviour, such as you might find among geniuses and lunatics.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about some magical power or treasure that exists in the world, it my be part of the Story or just a part of the background.
Revelations.Info_Description: Sometimes information can include Dooms, Mercari can get reads on other people's fate from time to time, and Prophets can do that for a whole people or region, or time-travellers may believe that they know exactly what is going to happen ahead of time. Of course, they aren't always right. Sometimes it is enough to know that the bad guy will get it in the very end, other times it isn't.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Intuition can be more than wild guesses. Sometimes subtle clues are not noticed by the conscious mind, but they tease at the subconscious, sometimes these clues emerge in dreams, but other times they present as gut instincts, or intuitions. For some Character's these hunches can be more valuable than another man's word, at least until they are proved wrong.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: Sometimes the information we get is Too Much Information, this might be that there are months of transcripts to work through, or that you now have detailed records of lavatory activities. There will be important information in there, if you can sift through the stuff you'd rather not know.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Magical details share information about the magic the

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked by the forces of history somehow. Perhaps some Færy Noble has been tasked to keep that route free as part of Wyrd's Tapestry for an hour, enter it, and the full forces of a powerful Yarn-Teller will be raised against you. It may not always be instant death... Although I'm not sure if that's meant to be reassuring, or terrifying.

Tarot.Normal: Magic On. Target has +2 Success Levels to all Magical Attacks, Defences, etc. while atop Pile. Additionally creates a "Magic" Reserve for the Target.
Tarot.Crossed: Magic Off. Target has +2 Failure Levels to all Magical Attacks, Defences, etc. while atop Pile. Additionally discards all "Magic" Reserves.

Ordeal.Obstacles: 2 Obstacles in every Non-Court Stage, 3 in every Court Stage
Ordeal.Stage_Description: A choice of three directions. A negotiation or a demonstration of power
Ordeal.Motional_Description: We're halting pursuit, Captain. Sir, yes I understand, but... Sir... Yessir... Okay, you heard the Captain, follow them into the minefield.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: This is it, the final choice that it all comes down to. Charge the Ebbing enemy, taking losses, and perhaps win, or wait for them regroup and mop you up. The Flowing forces must take at least a Maiming Wound to succeed during this Stage or they will become the Ebbing side.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The Fight is a major battle against a very powerful Monster. Sometimes a Force of Nature Monster with at least 2 ♣ Monster Facets, or even a Kaiju.
  Facet    Monster  
Burden ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Object — The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Boon. Objects are able increase the Scores of any rolled die and Pips of all played cards by their Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may add Burden Score cards to it’s Monster Pool, if it makes, or has made, modifications, or upgrades to itself, usually using components it has secured. It may add Burden Draw cards to its Monster Pool any time it is mistaken for a normal Object, or is used in accordance to its form or function. The Object Monster must play cards from its Monster Pool to perform any action that is not a part of its normal form and function.
Dominion ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Horde — The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. A Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their individual Values. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in number. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that potentially splits or reduces the Horde (like losing an individual or being attacked). If the Horde Monster Pool is emptied the Horde is scattered as individuals again, and must reform to regain Monster Pool. The Horde automatically adds the current Monster Pool Size (Number of cards) to pay any costs for multiple targets and may also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Pips to any perception-based Actions. E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1-3 cards in the Monster Pool granting them a bonus to offset any costs from multiple targets and a smaller bonus to perception) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual’s Value(45). If there were 12, or One million, Vexes would give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.
Fury ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Temperamental — The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as undying and shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is embodiment of berserk rage, and is almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience or induce their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When affecting a Character who is afflicted with their emotion they may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to their Actions. Temperamentals that are affected by emotional attacks must use cards from their Monster Pool to Ignore (Drawing equal to their Number of Active Hitches Reduced) the effect.
Rook ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Guardian — The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardian Monsters may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they interfere with another’s attack, or are attacked themselves. The Guardian will add Rook Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever they, or the thing they guard is Wounded or Damaged. A Guardian may spend a card from its Monster Pool to cancel another’s attack, this can be described as a dodge, or a soak. A Guardian may add a Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to any Attack or Defence action. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory. Guardians often use area-denial Splash attacks, such as breath weapons, tail lashes, wing buffeted dust, venom sprays, gaze attacks, and explosions, to drive enemies from their territory.
Sin ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Villain — The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (usually with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another, indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as “Evil” or as focused as “Smokes Gauloises Cigarettes” as you like. Villains may add +1 Score / Pips for each card in their Monster Pool to any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Sin Draw Monster Pool cards on creating Obstacles when they escape or are threatened. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.
Wyrd ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Doom-weaver — The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver Monster may draw and Prepare Monster Pool Size Reduced cards whenever anyone else plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards in the same Location. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, or even just powerful Mercari, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: Kings represent paths in the Labyrinth, and Cæster in the centre as well. A normal Bulmäs that tries to walk the Labyrinth would be lost, and would then be recruited into the Labyrinth. The Labyrinth can connect any point in space and time to any other, allowing Bulmäs to consciously time-travel through the Lea.  A King can allow a Bulmäs to create or reload a Chronolith or side-step depending on the power-level of the Bulmäs.

Stress.Description: Stress is dangerous, it can turn a simple mistake into a deadly danger, make a simple shock cause a heart attack, or make a Character hold a loaded gun to their own head.
Stress.Rules: All Clubs (♣) Suited Ordeal cards that target the Character for an Attack add the Character’s current Stress as Yarn so 13 Stress adds 66 Pips to each card, but then relieves Stress equal to the original Card Pips, plus all Strain and current Shock (although this new attack is a whole other ball game). Unsuited cards add current Stress as Chi (13 Pips), but remove only the current Stress Reduced (4 Stress), 1 Strain or 1 Shock.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can unlock abilities that the Character had no idea could even exist, let alone that they had access to them. Usually the Trauma will unlock some latent magical, or mutatagenic, power, this is not often a mental or emotional ability, but some physical effect usually. Of couse, genre and setting will greatly effect what "sorcery" can be unlocked by trauma. Of course, in certain genres and settings any useage of "Magic" can appear delusional, and even in fantasy settings some mages are considered crazy for the things they attempt with their magic.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma unlocks a Character’s "magical potential", typically this means that one of their Annexes can access "Magic" Boldly (as Chi). They will calculate Difficulty as though using magic (which is often easier). The Character with this Trauma can Relieve 1 Stress by laughing maniacally as they use their magic, regardless of the consequences.

Age.Description: An Age of Magic is occurring. During this age there is within a society some belief in magic. This is not necessarily a Mythic age that actually contains magic, although that can occur too, but more a rejection of materialistic mainstream knowledge, particuarly sciences. Magical thinking and beliefs may include fantastical or just misapplied mystical or scientific concepts, that can have positive and negative effects upon the society. Shared rituals, experiences and beliefs can draw a community closer together, but lead to irrational decision-making, social stagnation, and extremism.
Age.Rules: Magical thinking and beliefs are accepted within the society, often causing a rejection of materialistic scientific beliefs and counter-evidence. Often accompanied by a profound change in the society, often with a shift towards more popularist and extremist politics.
